output.summary: Added padding-top with var(--admin-bar-height) to the testimonial video modal to account for the WordPress admin bar, ensuring the video is visually centered in the viewport below the admin bar.

filesModified.purpose: Added pt-(--admin-bar-height) to video modal container
filesModified.changeDescription: Added `pt-(--admin-bar-height)` to the class list on the `.testimonial-video-modal` div. This shifts the flex centering area downward by the admin bar height so the video appears centered in the visible viewport area below the admin bar.

warnings: Asset size limit warnings (expected - editor.js 325 KiB, screen.js exceeds recommended 244 KiB limit)

syncStatus.notes: This is a PHP-only change. The video modal is not rendered in the TSX editor component, only on the frontend, so no TSX sync is required.

fixesApplied.description: Video modal was not accounting for WordPress admin bar height
fixesApplied.solution: Added padding-top using --admin-bar-height CSS variable (Tailwind v4 arbitrary property syntax)
fixesApplied.verificationMethod: The --admin-bar-height variable is defined in resources/css/screen/spacing.css and defaults to 0rem for logged-out users, so this is safe for all user states.

output.qaInstructions: Verify that when logged in as an admin with the WordPress admin bar visible, opening a testimonial video modal shows the video centered in the viewport below the admin bar, not obscured by it.
